title: "He Kore Tikanga: The Coalition of Betrayal" - 23 March 2026
tags: RNZ-Reid Research poll;National polls at 30.8 percent;collapsed to 17.3 percent;50 percent of New Zealanders now believe the country is headed in the wrong direction;Only 57.5 percent of ACT supporters think the country is on the right track;only 26.6 percent of NZ First voters feel the same;New Zealand's economy shrank sharply;73,400 New Zealand citizens left the country;Green Party documented what Budget 2025 did to Māori specifically;"little more than a politically motivated attack on perceived Māori privilege";"without any engagement or discussion with Māori";Justice Select Committee later called for it to be scrapped entirely;RNZ reported on legislation designed to make it harder for Māori to win coastal rights claims;1News documented in the councils' subsequent crisis of representation;Waitangi Tribunal launched an urgent inquiry into the referendums process;Te Ao Māori News reported;RNZ's analysis of the Privileges Committee found;Te Ao Māori News;a vibe problem;behind closed doors, in private meetings, away from public accountability;FamilyBoost scheme was labelled an "absolute disaster zone" by the Opposition;only 26.6 percent of its own voters thinking the country is on the right path;ACT sits at only 7 percent in the latest poll;poll result played out on election night, both blocs would receive 60 seats each;RNZ September 2025 poll had already pointed to a deadlocked parliament;Luxon has a preferred PM rating of 17.3 percent, with Erica Stanford registering 1.4 percent and Chris Bishop at 0.6 percent;RNZ's analyst notes;Waitangi Tribunal has said so;United Nations has been told;Green Party has documented the budget carnage;Koha — The Māori Green Lantern: Fighting Misinformation and Disinformation;The Māori Green Lantern — Subscribe
